a.xlink:title: Foundational formalism for temporally extended actions in reinforcement learning. Defines an option as a triple of initiation set, intra-option policy, and termination condition; treats the resulting ...

Hierarchical RL architecture with a high-level Manager that emits abstract goals in a learned latent space and a low-level Worker that is rewarded for moving along the Manager's directional goals. Dec...

Hierarchical cooperative MARL method that introduces dual coordination at both the high-level (across agents' subgoal selections) and low-level (across primitive actions), combined with QMIX-style val...
a.text: HAVEN (Hierarchical Cooperative Multi-Agent RL with Dual Coordination)

a.xlink:title: Quality-diversity algorithm that maintains a grid-shaped archive indexed by hand-designed behavior descriptors, keeping the highest-performing solution in each cell. Produces a diverse repertoire of l...

Black-box optimization of policy parameters using natural-evolution-strategy gradient estimates from antithetic Gaussian perturbations. Trades sample efficiency for trivial parallelism: the per-worker...
a.text: OpenAI ES (Evolution Strategies as a Scalable Alternative to RL)

a.xlink:title: Unsupervised environment design that drops the adversary network in favor of an evolutionary edit operator: a buffer of high-regret levels is mutated and re-scored, compounding complexity along the ag...
a.text: ACCEL (Adversarially Compounding Complexity by Editing Levels)

a.xlink:title: Trains a reward model from pairwise human preference comparisons over trajectory snippets, then optimizes a policy against that reward via standard deep RL. Foundational anchor for the entire human-fe...

Anthropic's recipe for aligning an LLM with a written 'constitution' of principles using AI feedback instead of human labels: the model critiques and revises its own outputs against the principles, pr...

Reformulates RLHF as a closed-form classification objective on pairwise preferences, eliminating the explicit reward model and PPO loop. Proves that the optimal policy under a KL-constrained reward mo...

Three-part architecture: a Variational Autoencoder compresses observations, a recurrent mixture-density network predicts latent dynamics, and a small linear controller is evolved by CMA-ES inside the ...

Combines a learned value-equivalent dynamics model with Monte Carlo Tree Search, removing the need for a known simulator at planning time. Matches AlphaZero on Go / chess / shogi while also achieving ...

Latent-space world model that learns from pixels and trains an actor-critic entirely on imagined trajectories. Famously masters Minecraft diamonds from scratch with a single fixed hyperparameter set a...

Multi-agent latent world-model method in the Dreamer family: agents jointly maintain a shared latent state via learned communication and train policies on imagined rollouts of that joint model. Demons...

Grandmaster-level StarCraft II player from DeepMind. Population-based self-play with a 'league' of main agents, main exploiters, and league exploiters to prevent strategy cycles. Influential for the p...

Unsupervised environment design framework: a protagonist agent and an antagonist agent both play environments produced by an adversary, and the adversary is trained to maximize the regret between them...
a.text: PAIRED (Protagonist Antagonist Induced Regret Environment Design)

a.xlink:title: Multi-agent extension of PAIRED: jointly co-evolves a population of co-players and a curriculum of environments to maximize regret in cooperative MARL. Targets the failure mode where standard environm...

Embodied LLM-agent that continually consumes natural-language environment feedback (success detection, scene description, human input) to drive an 'inner monologue' that revises plans on the fly. Show...

Self-reflective agent: after each trial, the LLM verbalizes what went wrong and stores the reflection in episodic memory for the next attempt. Variant of 'RL via verbal feedback' where the policy upda...

Lifelong-learning LLM agent in Minecraft. Builds an ever-growing skill library via iterative prompting (propose tasks, write code, execute, reflect) without gradient updates. Demonstrates that LLM age...

Grounds an LLM planner in a robot's actual affordances by combining 'Say' (the LLM's likelihood that a skill helps the goal) with 'Can' (a learned value function for whether the robot can execute that...

Interleaves Reasoning and Acting in LLM agents: the model alternates between generating verbal reasoning traces and external tool actions. Foundational pattern for the agentic-AI wave; nearly every LL...

Frames LLM problem-solving as deliberate search over a tree of partial 'thoughts': at each node, the model proposes candidate next steps, an evaluator scores them, and a search strategy (BFS / DFS) ex...

Microsoft's framework for multi-agent LLM applications: define agents with roles (UserProxy, AssistantAgent, GroupChatManager) that exchange messages in natural language to solve user tasks. The agent...

First systematic evaluation suite for LLM-as-agent across eight environments spanning operating systems, databases, knowledge graphs, card games, household tasks, web browsing, web shopping, and digit...

DeepMind benchmark suite of multi-agent reinforcement learning environments organized around social dilemmas, free-rider problems, common-pool resources, and other mixed-motive scenarios. The most pro...

Foundational decentralized MARL approach: each agent runs independent Q-learning, treating other agents as part of the environment dynamics. Trivial to implement and scale; pays for it in non-stationa...

Earliest deep value-decomposition method: joint Q-function is the simple sum of per-agent Q utilities. Trivially satisfies Individual-Global-Max. Limited expressiveness compared to QMIX's hypernetwork...

Independent PPO with parameter sharing: each agent runs PPO on its own observations and treats others as part of the environment. Surprisingly strong on cooperative benchmarks despite lacking centrali...

Formal complexity-theoretic anchor for cooperative MARL: defines the decentralized POMDP and proves that finite-horizon optimal policies are NEXP-complete, ruling out tractable exact algorithms in gen...
a.text: Dec-POMDP (Complexity of Decentralized Control of Markov Decision Processes)

a.xlink:title: Multi-Agent DDPG: centralized critics for each agent (sees all observations and actions during training) with decentralized actors (each agent's policy uses only its own observation). Established the ...

Solves multi-agent credit assignment by computing a counterfactual baseline: for each agent, the centralized critic estimates how much that agent's specific action contributed beyond a default. Strong...

Factorizes the joint Q-function as a monotonic mixture of per-agent Q utilities, enforced via a hypernetwork with non-negative weights. Satisfies the Individual-Global-Max condition so per-agent argma...

Recasts a cooperative Dec-POMDP with hidden information as a public-belief MDP: agents condition on a Bayesian posterior over private states given the public history, allowing decentralized policies t...

Centralized-Training Decentralized-Execution variant of PPO: a centralized value function uses global state, decentralized actors use only per-agent observations, and agents share parameters. Empirica...

Introduces Markov games (stochastic games) as the formalism for competitive multi-agent reinforcement learning and proposes minimax-Q, a value-iteration variant that converges to optimal policies in t...

Foundational learned-communication architecture for cooperative MARL: at each step, every agent's hidden state is mean-pooled across all agents and broadcast back as the next-layer input, yielding a d...

First gradient-based learned-communication method: during centralized training, agents pass continuous messages with gradients flowing through the channel; at decentralized execution, messages are dis...

Models the team policy and Q-function as a bidirectional recurrent network whose hidden states act as an inter-agent communication channel, applied to StarCraft 1 micro-management. Demonstrates that r...

Adds a learned binary gate to CommNet so each agent decides at each step whether to broadcast at all, addressing the 'always talk' assumption of CommNet. Demonstrates that learning when to communicate...

Replaces CommNet's mean-pool broadcast with signature-based soft attention: each agent emits a (key, value) pair, and listeners use queries to compute attention weights, learning who to address and wh...

Combines a Scheduler (which decides when and to whom to communicate) with a Message Processor based on Graph Attention Networks operating over a dynamically-learned communication graph. Unifies the 'w...

Robust adversarial extension of MADDPG that trains each agent's policy against an adversarially perturbed approximation of the others, framed as a minimax objective on the centralized critic. Uses a o...

Builds an opponent model jointly with each agent's policy under a maximum entropy RL objective, then regularizes the policy update against the inferred opponent posterior. Yields a Bayes-optimal best-...
a.text: ROMMEO (Regularized Opponent Model with Maximum Entropy Objective)

a.xlink:title: Lightweight 2D continuous-state particle environment introduced alongside MADDPG, with a small suite of cooperative, competitive, and mixed-motive tasks (cooperative navigation, predator-prey, speaker...

Benchmark suite of cooperative micromanagement scenarios in StarCraft II with 2-27 unit agents, decentralized partial observations, and a fixed scripted enemy. The de-facto cooperative-MARL benchmark ...

Approximates the joint action of a large agent population by the empirical distribution (mean field) of neighbor actions, reducing the multi-agent Q-function to a pairwise interaction with a populatio...

Augments QMIX with a hierarchical latent variable that conditions the joint policy and is trained via mutual-information maximization. Encourages diverse coordinated exploration strategies, addressing...

Replaces QMIX's monotonic mixing with a soft regularization of the Individual-Global-Max condition, making the function class strictly broader. Strongest theoretical guarantees in the value-decomposit...

Combines QMIX-style value decomposition with an information-theoretic regularizer that minimizes mutual information between sent messages and the sender's full observation while preserving task-releva...

Augments value-decomposition MARL with a stochastic role embedding per agent, regularized so roles are mutually informative with trajectories but compact enough to drive specialization. Roles conditio...

Duplex dueling decomposition with multi-head attention over agents. Generalizes QMIX's monotonic mixing to handle non-monotonic interactions while still satisfying IGM. Outperforms QMIX and QTRAN on S...

Replaces the per-agent recurrent encoder in value-decomposition MARL with an entity-based transformer: each agent attends over a variable-length set of observed entities, and the policy head decouples...

Defeated world-champion human Dota 2 team using PPO at unprecedented scale. Five LSTM agents share parameters and receive a cooperative team reward. Demonstrated that scale + self-play could reach pro...

Treats agents as nodes in a graph defined by spatial neighborhood and applies multi-head graph-attention layers to fuse local observations before Q-learning. Demonstrates that GNN-based message passin...

Learns an implicit coordination graph end-to-end: an attention module produces a soft adjacency that feeds a GNN reasoning layer over joint values or actions. Operates in both centralized-execution an...

Recasts cooperative MARL as a sequence-modeling problem: an encoder-decoder transformer encodes the joint observation and decodes agent actions one at a time, leveraging the multi-agent advantage deco...

Multi-agent collaborative framework that structures problem-solving into four phases: expert recruitment, collaborative decision-making, action execution, and evaluation. The framework dynamically ass...

LLM-agent virtual software company with role-based agents communicating via structured chat protocols. Distinguishes itself from MetaGPT through explicit 'double-agent' debate phases at each stage (de...

Software-development LLM-agent system encoding standard operating procedures from human teams: a Product Manager writes requirements, an Architect designs, Engineers implement, QA tests. Demonstrates ...

Open-source Python framework for orchestrating role-playing autonomous LLM agents organized as a 'crew': each agent has a role, goal, backstory, and tools, and tasks are distributed across the crew ei...

Library from LangChain for building stateful multi-agent LLM workflows as explicit directed graphs with shared state. Nodes are LLM agents or tool calls, edges are conditional transitions, and persist...

Two-agent role-playing framework: an 'AI user' and an 'AI assistant' are given complementary roles via 'inception prompting' and exchange messages to solve a task. One of the earliest published multi-...

Multiple LLM agents propose, critique, and refine each other's answers through structured debate rounds. Improves factual accuracy and reasoning on math/QA tasks beyond single-agent baselines. Explici...

Models multi-agent decision making as recursive level-K reasoning where each agent assumes opponents reason one level shallower. Uses variational inference to approximate the joint policy and derives ...

Differentiates each agent's update through an explicit one-step lookahead on the opponent's learning step, so policies are shaped by how they will influence the opponent's future gradient. Yields tit-...

Meta AI's human-level Diplomacy player: combines a planning module trained via no-press self-play with a dialogue model fine-tuned on human games and conditioned on intended actions. Demonstrates that...

Adds an intrinsic reward proportional to one agent's causal influence on another's policy (KL between conditional and marginal action distributions) to encourage emergent communication and prosocial b...

Two-level RL framework where a 'social planner' agent designs tax policy while heterogeneous worker agents simultaneously learn to respond. Uses PPO at both levels to discover tax schedules that impro...

25 LLM-driven generative agents in a sandbox town (Smallville) exhibit believable individual and social behavior using a memory stream, reflection, and planning module. Demonstrated emergent social ph...

Open-source deployable virtual town inspired by the Generative Agents paper: LLM-driven characters live, plan, gossip, and form relationships in a Convex-backed real-time game world. Provides a practi...
